nodes.definition: The existence of a smallest measurable distance ~ell_Pl, a shared feature of string theory (string length ell_s), loop quantum gravity (discrete area), and noncommutative geometry. Phenomenologically encoded via GUP and modified dispersion relations.

nodes.name: Planck Scale
nodes.definition: The natural quantum-gravity scale: E_Pl = sqrt(hbar c^5/G) ~ 1.22e19 GeV, ell_Pl ~ 1.6e-35 m, t_Pl ~ 5.4e-44 s. ~15 orders of magnitude above LHC, motivating amplification strategies (cosmological distance, high energy, macroscopic quantum coherence) in phenomenology.

nodes.name: Gravitational Wave Echoes
nodes.definition: Repeated pulses following ringdown, predicted if the classical horizon is replaced by quantum structure (firewall, fuzzball, exotic compact object) reflecting waves at ~ell_Pl from the horizon. Searched for in LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA data; no statistically significant detection, first model-independent late-time QNM bounds set.

nodes.name: Ringdown / Black-Hole Spectroscopy
nodes.definition: Measurement of quasinormal-mode (QNM) frequencies after a binary black-hole merger, testing the Kerr metric and the no-hair theorem and constraining beyond-GR (quadratic gravity, dynamical Chern-Simons, scalar-Gauss-Bonnet). GW250114 (O4) provided a high-SNR ringdown consistent with GR.

nodes.name: Gravitational Wave Memory
nodes.definition: Permanent shift in the relative position of test masses after a GW passes; mathematically the Fourier transform of Weinberg's soft graviton theorem (Strominger-Zhiboedov). Its detection would test the soft-graviton/BMS-supertranslation structure underlying scattering amplitudes.

nodes.name: Soft Theorem / BMS Supertranslations
nodes.definition: The memory-soft-symmetry triangle: GW memory = soft graviton theorem = BMS supertranslation Ward identity. A deep link between an observable GW phenomenon and the asymptotic symmetries and soft structure of gravitational scattering amplitudes.

nodes.name: Graviton Mass Bound
nodes.definition: A nonzero graviton mass m_g modifies GW dispersion E^2 = p^2 c^2 + m_g^2 c^4, causing frequency-dependent delay in the waveform. LIGO/Virgo (GWTC-3) bounds m_g <~ 1.3e-23 eV/c^2; GW170817-GRB170817A coincidence gives |v_g - c|/c <~ 1e-15.

nodes.name: Single-Graviton Detection
nodes.definition: Gravito-phononic analog of the photoelectric effect: stimulated absorption of a single graviton in a macroscopic quantum resonator, resolvable with quantum sensing (Tobar et al. 2024). A possible first experimental clue of gravity quantization, though stimulated processes also occur classically.

nodes.name: Atom Interferometry / MAGIS-100
nodes.definition: A 100 m matter-wave atomic gradiometer (Fermilab, NuMI shaft) using ultracold strontium to search for ultralight (wave-like) dark matter, test quantum mechanics on macroscopic scales and the equivalence principle, and pathfind mid-band (0.1-10 Hz) GW detection. Laser lab completed 2026; multi-year commissioning.

nodes.name: Levitated Optomechanics
nodes.definition: Levitated nanoparticles/nanodiamonds cooled to the motional ground state in optical/magnetic/electric traps; the leading route to high-mass quantum superpositions (10^6 amu and above) needed for BMV-type tests, with electron diffraction giving ~1000x larger momentum splitting than two-photon recoil.

nodes.name: Standard-Model Extension (SME)
nodes.definition: An effective field theory framework cataloging all Lorentz- and CPT-violating operators by mass dimension, providing the standard parametrization (coefficients) against which GRB, UHECR, neutrino, and laboratory LIV bounds are reported.

nodes.name: Geontropic Noise (Verlinde-Zurek effect)
nodes.definition: Predicted correlated fluctuation of an interferometer arm length arising from fluctuations of the modular (entanglement) energy of holographic degrees of freedom on the boundary of a causal diamond: <Delta K^2> = <K> gives <Delta L^2> ~ ell_Pl L with a characteristic (non-flat, IR-dominated) spectral shape. Unlike Hogan's flat holographic noise it can evade Holometer bounds; targeted by the GQuEST single-photon interferometer. A concrete experimental window onto the 'spacetime from entanglement' program.

nodes.name: Tensor Model
nodes.definition: A 0-dimensional probabilistic/combinatorial model for a rank-d tensor T_{a_1...a_d} with indices a_i in {1,...,N}, generalizing random matrix models (d=2) to higher rank. Generates random triangulations of d-dimensional pseudomanifolds as Feynman graphs; the combinatorial 'skeleton' of GFT without geometric (group) data.

nodes.name: Matrix Model of 2D Gravity
nodes.definition: Integral over N x N Hermitian matrices whose Feynman graphs are dual to random triangulated 2D surfaces, organized by a genus (topological) expansion F ~ sum_g N^{2-2g} F_g. In the double-scaling limit it provides a non-perturbative definition of 2D quantum gravity / 2D string theory; pure gravity is governed by a Painleve I equation.

nodes.name: Colored Tensor Model
nodes.definition: Gurau's variant with d+1 distinct fields ('colors'), each vertex containing exactly one field of each color. Coloring guarantees that dual complexes are simplicial pseudomanifolds in any dimension and enables a well-defined topological 1/N expansion via the Gurau degree.

nodes.name: Gurau Degree
nodes.definition: A non-negative integer omega assigned to a colored tensor graph, defined as the sum of genera of its jackets (globally embedded ribbon subgraphs). It plays the role that genus plays for matrix models, controlling the 1/N expansion via the power N^{d - (2/(d-1)!) omega}. It is a combinatorial invariant of the graph, not a topological invariant of the dual manifold.

nodes.name: Melonic Dominance
nodes.definition: The phenomenon that graphs of Gurau degree omega=0 ('melons', dual to triangulations of the sphere S^d, built by iterated insertion of pairs of vertices joined by d parallel edges) dominate the leading order of the large-N / 1/N expansion. Melonic sums are explicitly resummable and yield closed Schwinger-Dyson (gap) equations. Shared by tensor models and the SYK model.

nodes.name: 1/N Expansion
nodes.definition: Systematic topological expansion of free energy / correlators in inverse powers of the index range N, organizing the sum over geometries by suppressing more complicated topologies. For matrices organized by genus; for tensors by the Gurau degree; for SYK/tensor QM by a melonic limit (e.g. g^2 N^3 fixed).

nodes.name: Double Scaling Limit
nodes.definition: A coordinated limit of large index N together with tuning the coupling to its critical value, retaining all orders of the topological expansion to give a non-perturbative result. For matrix models yields 2D gravity (Painleve I); for tensor models in D<6 it is stable and resums melons plus subleading 'cherry trees'.

nodes.name: Boulatov-Ooguri Model
nodes.definition: The original GFT: Boulatov (1992) defined a field theory over three copies of SU(2) (with gauge invariance) whose Feynman amplitudes reproduce the Ponzano-Regge model of 3D gravity; Ooguri (1992) extended it to 4D BF theory over four copies of SU(2). Backbone of all geometric GFT models.

nodes.name: Spin Foam
nodes.definition: A 2-complex with faces carrying group irreducible representations and edges carrying intertwiners; the 'history' of a spin network. GFT Feynman amplitudes equal spin-foam amplitudes, so GFT is the generating functional that also sums over the underlying complexes and topologies.

nodes.name: Second Quantization of LQG
nodes.definition: Reformulation in which GFT fields are creation/annihilation operators for 'atoms of space' (open spin-network nodes dual to d-simplices). The Fock space of many such quanta reproduces the LQG kinematical Hilbert space; the GFT action encodes the canonical LQG dynamics.

nodes.name: GFT Condensate
nodes.definition: A coherent / squeezed state of a large number of GFT quanta in the same single-particle geometric configuration, analogous to a Bose-Einstein condensate ('atoms of space'). A macroscopically homogeneous condensate describes a homogeneous quantum cosmology; its hydrodynamics yields effective cosmological dynamics.

nodes.name: Effective Friedmann Equation (GFT)
nodes.definition: A generalization of the Friedmann equations derived from GFT condensate hydrodynamics in the Gross-Pitaevskii approximation, including quantum-gravity corrections. For a single-spin condensate (V'/3V)^2 = (4 pi G/3)(1 - rho/rho_c) + ..., recovering classical GR for rho << rho_c and producing a bounce at rho = rho_c.

nodes.name: Quantum Bounce
nodes.definition: Resolution of the big-bang/big-crunch singularity: the GFT condensate volume reaches a non-zero turning point (V'=0) at a critical Planckian density rho_c, replacing the singularity by a bounce. A repulsive divergent term -Q^2/rho^3 prevents the condensate amplitude from vanishing. Qualitatively matches loop quantum cosmology.

nodes.name: Relational Time (Clock Field)
nodes.definition: A minimally coupled massless scalar field phi used as a physical clock; the GFT condensate dynamics and effective Friedmann equations are expressed relationally with respect to phi (derivatives d/dphi), bypassing the problem of time and the absence of background coordinates.

nodes.name: Tensorial Group Field Theory (TGFT)
nodes.definition: GFT/tensor field theory with tensorial (combinatorially non-trivial, non-melonically-bonded) interactions, suited to renormalization and FRG analysis; may include both non-local group degrees of freedom and local (scalar/clock) degrees of freedom. Exhibits asymptotic freedom and Wilson-Fisher-type fixed points.

nodes.name: GFT/TGFT Renormalization
nodes.definition: Adaptation of QFT renormalization (multiscale analysis, power counting, Wick ordering by subtracting melonic tadpoles) to GFT/TGFT. Rank-4 tensor field theory is renormalizable to all orders; 4D Abelian U(1) TGFT with gauge invariance is super-renormalizable for any polynomial interaction.

nodes.name: Asymptotic Freedom (TGFT)
nodes.definition: Generic feature of renormalizable tensorial GFTs: the leading (melonic) coupling has beta function beta(lambda) ~ -lambda^3, so the coupling decreases in the UV. Wavefunction renormalization at one loop is crucial. Coexists with IR Wilson-Fisher-type fixed points.

nodes.name: Phase Transition / Geometrogenesis Transition
nodes.definition: Proposal that continuum spacetime emerges as a phase (condensate or critical point) of the underlying GFT system, reached via a quantum-gravity analogue of a thermodynamic limit. Landau-Ginzburg mean-field and FRG analyses determine critical dimensions and conditions for a continuous transition.

nodes.name: Geometrogenesis / Emergence of Spacetime
nodes.definition: The hypothesis that smooth, continuum spacetime is not fundamental but emerges from a large number of pre-geometric, non-spatiotemporal GFT quanta, analogous to a condensed-matter system (a 'spacetime fluid'). Spacetime emergence is decoupled from the ontology of the fundamental entities.

nodes.name: Melonic Schwinger-Dyson (Gap) Equation
nodes.definition: Self-consistent equation for the two-point function in a melonically dominated model: G = 1/(K - Sigma) with self-energy Sigma(t1,t2) = g^2 [G(t1,t2)]^{q-1}. In the IR (kinetic term dropped) it admits a conformal solution G ~ |t|^{-2Delta} with Delta=1/q, giving emergent conformal symmetry shared by SYK and tensor models.

nodes.name: Ponzano-Regge Model
nodes.definition: State-sum model for 3D Euclidean quantum gravity assigning SU(2) 6j-symbols to tetrahedra of a triangulated 3-manifold. The Boulatov GFT generates its triangulations and amplitudes as Feynman diagrams.

nodes.name: Barrett-Crane Model
nodes.definition: A 4D spin-foam model of quantum gravity obtained by imposing simplicity constraints on BF theory; arises from a Boulatov-Ooguri GFT over the homogeneous space SO(4)/SO(3) (Euclidean) and admits a complete Lorentzian version with spacelike, timelike and lightlike tetrahedra encoding local causal structure.

nodes.name: Bekenstein Bound
nodes.definition: Universal upper bound on the entropy of a physical system of energy E enclosed in a sphere of radius R: S <= 2 pi k R E / (hbar c). Saturated by a black hole; implies finite information content in finite regions.

nodes.name: Covariant Entropy Bound (Bousso Bound)
nodes.definition: Generalized holographic bound (Bousso 1999): entropy on a light sheet (congruence of non-expanding null rays orthogonal to a surface B of area A) satisfies S <= A/4G. Valid in general spacetimes including cosmology, where the spacelike entropy bound fails.

nodes.name: AdS/CFT Correspondence
nodes.definition: Exact duality (Maldacena 1997) between quantum gravity / string theory on (d+1)-dimensional anti-de Sitter space and a conformal field theory without gravity on its d-dimensional conformal boundary. Prototype: type IIB strings on AdS5 x S5 dual to N=4 super-Yang-Mills with gauge group SU(N).

nodes.name: GKP-Witten Dictionary
nodes.definition: Precise prescription (Gubser-Klebanov-Polyakov and Witten 1998): the CFT generating functional with boundary source phi_0 equals the bulk gravity partition function with that boundary condition, Z_CFT[phi_0] = Z_grav[phi -> phi_0]. Each CFT operator maps to a bulk field; correlators are computed from the on-shell gravity action (Witten diagrams).

nodes.name: Field-Operator Map / Mass-Dimension Relation
nodes.definition: Dictionary entry relating a bulk field's mass m to the conformal dimension Delta of the dual boundary operator: Delta(Delta - d) = m^2 L^2. Light fields map to low-dimension (relevant) operators; the Breitenlohner-Freedman bound m^2 L^2 >= -d^2/4 allows stable tachyonic fields.

nodes.name: 't Hooft Limit and Coupling
nodes.definition: Large-N planar limit with lambda = g_YM^2 N fixed as N -> infinity. In AdS5/CFT4: L^4/alpha'^2 = 4 pi g_s N = g_YM^2 N = lambda. Strong CFT coupling (lambda >> 1) maps to classical bulk gravity; weak coupling to highly curved stringy geometry.

nodes.name: Holographic RG Flow
nodes.definition: Identification of the bulk radial coordinate with the boundary energy scale: motion into the AdS interior is RG flow toward the IR. Domain-wall (kink) solutions interpolating between AdS vacua of different radius realize RG flows between fixed points; the holographic c-theorem follows from the null energy condition.

nodes.name: Ryu-Takayanagi Formula
nodes.definition: Holographic entanglement entropy: the entanglement entropy of a boundary region equals Area(gamma)/(4G) for the minimal bulk surface gamma homologous to it. Geometrizes entanglement, connects spacetime geometry to quantum information, and underlies the 'spacetime from entanglement' program.

nodes.name: Bulk Reconstruction (HKLL)
nodes.definition: Procedure (Hamilton-Kabat-Lifschytz-Lowe) expressing a local bulk operator as an integral of boundary CFT operators against a smearing function supported on boundary points spacelike-separated from the bulk insertion. Reveals the bulk's quantum-error-correcting structure.

nodes.name: Holographic Quantum Error Correction
nodes.definition: Almheiri-Dong-Harlow insight that AdS/CFT realizes operator-algebra quantum error correction: bulk (logical) information is redundantly encoded on the boundary (physical) qubits and protected against erasure of large boundary regions. Modeled by tensor networks (HaPPY code).

nodes.name: Subregion / Entanglement-Wedge Duality
nodes.definition: A boundary subregion's density matrix encodes (and allows reconstruction of) the bulk physics within its entanglement wedge, the bulk region bounded by the RT/HRT surface. Sharpens locality of the holographic map.

nodes.name: Tensor Network (Holographic)
nodes.definition: Discrete networks of tensors (MERA, HaPPY perfect-tensor codes, random tensor networks) that reproduce key holographic features: RT formula, negative tripartite information, error-correcting bulk reconstruction. Toy models of emergent geometry from entanglement.

nodes.name: dS/CFT and de Sitter Holography
nodes.definition: Conjectured duality (Strominger 2001) between gravity on de Sitter space and a non-unitary Euclidean CFT on the future spacelike boundary; alternative static-patch holography places dual degrees of freedom on the stretched horizon. Far less understood than AdS/CFT.

nodes.name: Celestial Holography
nodes.definition: Program recasting the 4D gravitational S-matrix in asymptotically flat space as correlators of a 2D celestial CFT on the celestial sphere. Soft theorems become Ward identities of infinite-dimensional asymptotic (BMS) symmetries; celestial amplitudes are Mellin transforms of momentum-space amplitudes.

nodes.name: Soft Theorems / Memory / BMS
nodes.definition: Infrared triangle of asymptotically flat gravity: soft graviton theorems, gravitational memory effect, and BMS (super-translation / super-rotation) asymptotic symmetries are equivalent. They underpin celestial holography and the celestial symmetry algebra (w_{1+infinity}).

nodes.name: BMS Asymptotic Symmetry
nodes.definition: Bondi-Metzner-Sachs symmetry: the infinite-dimensional asymptotic symmetry group of asymptotically flat spacetime at null infinity (supertranslations and superrotations). Matching conditions between past and future null infinity constrain gravitational scattering and underlie celestial holography.

nodes.name: Carrollian Holography
nodes.definition: Holography of flat / de Sitter space formulated via Carrollian CFTs (the c -> 0 ultra-relativistic limit of conformal symmetry) living on null infinity or the dS boundary; complementary to the celestial (Lorentzian 2D CFT) approach.

nodes.name: Integrability of Planar N=4 SYM
nodes.definition: Planar N=4 super-Yang-Mills is integrable: its one-loop dilatation operator is the Hamiltonian of an integrable spin chain, and the Quantum Spectral Curve gives exact anomalous dimensions at any 't Hooft coupling, providing the most precise quantitative tests of AdS/CFT.

nodes.name: Quantum Spectral Curve
nodes.definition: Integrability-based framework giving the exact planar spectrum of N=4 SYM (and the dual string) as the solution of a Riemann-Hilbert problem for a finite set of Q-functions; interpolates from weak to strong coupling.

nodes.name: Supersymmetric Localization
nodes.definition: Technique (Pestun 2007) reducing supersymmetric partition functions and Wilson loops on spheres to finite-dimensional matrix integrals, computable exactly at finite coupling; yields exact AdS/CFT checks such as the N^{3/2} free energy of ABJM.

nodes.name: Conformal Bootstrap
nodes.definition: Nonperturbative program constraining CFT data using crossing symmetry and unitarity; analytic (Lorentzian inversion formula) and numerical versions test and constrain holographic CFTs, with bulk locality emerging from large-spin/heavy-operator structure.

nodes.name: KSS Viscosity Bound
nodes.definition: Holographic lower bound eta/s >= hbar/(4 pi k_B) on the shear-viscosity-to-entropy-density ratio (Kovtun-Son-Starinets), saturated in Einstein gravity. Quark-gluon plasma at RHIC/LHC sits near this bound (eta/s ~ 0.1-0.3), the 'most perfect fluid'.

nodes.name: Holographic Quark-Gluon Plasma
nodes.definition: Application of AdS/CFT to the strongly coupled quark-gluon plasma produced in heavy-ion collisions; holography predicts near-perfect-fluid transport (eta/s ~ 1/4 pi), jet quenching, and thermalization, qualitatively matching RHIC/LHC data.

nodes.name: AdS/CMT (Holographic Condensed Matter)
nodes.definition: Application of gauge/gravity duality to strongly correlated condensed-matter systems without quasiparticles: holographic superconductors, strange metals, non-Fermi liquids, and momentum-relaxing transport, modeled by charged AdS black holes at finite density.

nodes.name: SYK Model / JT Gravity / Nearly-AdS2
nodes.definition: The Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model (N Majorana fermions, all-to-all random couplings) is maximally chaotic and holographically dual to Jackiw-Teitelboim gravity in nearly-AdS2; the Schwarzian action captures the soft modes. A solvable laboratory for near-extremal black holes, chaos, and the gravitational path integral.

nodes.name: Wedge / Double Holography
nodes.definition: Holography for a wedge bounded by end-of-the-world branes (Karch-Randall braneworld), admitting equivalent bulk, brane, and boundary descriptions; central to deriving the Page curve with gravitating baths and to constructing de Sitter braneworlds.

nodes.name: Black Hole Microstate Counting
nodes.definition: Statistical-mechanical origin of S = A/4: Strominger-Vafa counted BPS D-brane bound states reproducing the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy of extremal 5D black holes; Cardy formula reproduces BTZ entropy. Microstates of generic non-extremal black holes remain open.

nodes.name: Holographic c-Theorem
nodes.definition: Monotonicity of a c-function along RG flow proven holographically: the gravity dual is the second law for causal horizons, and the holographic c-function is the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy density of the dynamical causal horizon.

nodes.name: Factorization / Baby-Universe Problem
nodes.definition: The gravitational path integral includes wormholes that make the two-copy partition function fail to factorize, in tension with a factorizing dual QFT; resolution may require ensemble averaging (as in JT/SYK) and alpha-states, unclear for unique theories like N=4 SYM.

nodes.name: T-Tbar Deformation
nodes.definition: Solvable irrelevant deformation of a 2D CFT by the dimension-4 T-Tbar operator; holographically it places the boundary theory on a Dirichlet wall at finite bulk radius, realizing a finite cutoff and connecting to dS/dS holography. The RG flow equation matches the radial Hamilton-Jacobi equation of bulk gravity.

nodes.name: Light Sheet
nodes.definition: Central geometric object of the Bousso bound: a null hypersurface generated by a congruence of non-expanding light rays emitted orthogonally from a surface; its entropy flux is bounded by the surface area over 4G.

nodes.name: Ashtekar-Barbero variables
nodes.definition: Canonical reformulation of general relativity in terms of an SU(2) connection A_a^i = Gamma_a^i + gamma K_a^i and a densitized triad E^a_i, recasting gravity as a gauge theory with a Yang-Mills-like phase space. They make the constraints polynomial and form the starting point for canonical LQG.

nodes.name: Holonomy-flux algebra
nodes.definition: The fundamental kinematical algebra of LQG built from holonomies of the connection along edges and fluxes of the triad through surfaces, replacing pointwise fields. By the LOST/Fleischhack theorem it admits a unique diffeomorphism-invariant cyclic representation.

nodes.name: LOST/Fleischhack uniqueness theorem
nodes.definition: Theorem (Lewandowski-Okolow-Sahlmann-Thiemann; Fleischhack) that the holonomy-flux algebra has a unique diffeomorphism-invariant state, hence a unique kinematical representation (the Ashtekar-Lewandowski representation). It gives LQG kinematic rigidity.

nodes.name: Spin network
nodes.definition: A graph embedded in a spatial slice with edges labeled by SU(2) irreps (spins j) and vertices by intertwiners; spin networks form an orthonormal basis of the kinematical Hilbert space. Edges carry quanta of area and vertices quanta of volume.

nodes.name: Discrete area spectrum
nodes.definition: The LQG area operator has a discrete spectrum A = 8 pi gamma l_P^2 sum sqrt(j(j+1)), so surfaces have a minimum nonzero quantum of area (area gap). A signature prediction of quantized geometry.

nodes.name: Discrete volume spectrum
nodes.definition: The LQG volume operator has a discrete spectrum with contributions from spin-network vertices of valence >= 4 with non-coplanar geometry, giving space a minimum meaningful volume. No closed-form general spectrum exists.

nodes.name: Quantum geometry
nodes.definition: The picture, emerging from discrete area/volume spectra, in which space has an atomic, granular structure at the Planck scale, with spin networks describing quantum states of 3-geometry.

nodes.name: Barbero-Immirzi parameter
nodes.definition: A dimensionless free parameter gamma entering the Ashtekar-Barbero connection. Classically a canonical-transformation label (does not affect classical dynamics), but quantum-mechanically it sets the scale of discrete geometry and black hole entropy; fixed by matching S = A/4.

nodes.name: EPRL-FK spin foam model
nodes.definition: The modern 4D Lorentzian spin foam model (Engle-Pereira-Rovelli-Livine / Freidel-Krasnov) obtained by imposing linear simplicity constraints p = gamma j, k = j on BF theory via the Y-map embedding SU(2) irreps into SL(2,C). Reproduces the Regge action in the large-spin limit.

nodes.name: BF theory / Plebanski formulation
nodes.definition: A topological field theory (action B wedge F) from which gravity is recovered by imposing simplicity constraints forcing B = *(e wedge e). Spin foams realize this 'constrained BF' strategy by discretizing the path integral.

nodes.name: Y-map / linear simplicity constraint
nodes.definition: The map Y_gamma embedding SU(2) spin-j states into the SL(2,C) principal series (p = gamma j, k = j), implementing the simplicity (Plebanski) constraint at the quantum level and linking the canonical (SU(2)) and covariant (Lorentzian) theories.

nodes.name: Spin foam partition function / transition amplitude
nodes.definition: Z = sum over labelings of products of face, edge and vertex amplitudes on a 2-complex, providing the covariant dynamics (a sum over geometries) and ideally a projector onto the physical Hilbert space of canonical LQG.

nodes.name: Flatness problem of spin foams
nodes.definition: A pathology whereby the semiclassical (large-spin) limit of spin-foam amplitudes can be dominated by flat geometries, failing to reproduce full curved Regge dynamics; numerically confirmed in BF and EPRL cases.

nodes.name: Effective spin foam models
nodes.definition: Computationally efficient spin-foam models (Asante-Dittrich-Haggard) using the quantum area spectrum, strongly imposed gluing constraints and the discrete GR action; they avoid the flatness problem in a restricted parameter regime and enable first semiclassical tests.

nodes.name: Numerical spin foams (sl2cfoam-next)
nodes.definition: High-performance computation of Lorentzian EPRL amplitudes (sl2cfoam-next code) plus Monte Carlo sampling, enabling tests of single-vertex asymptotics, the flatness problem and the first explicit spin-foam dynamics with an internal edge.

nodes.name: Spin foam coarse-graining and renormalization
nodes.definition: Tensor-network and renormalization-group methods applied to spin foams to study refinement/continuum limits and phase structure, since Monte Carlo is obstructed by complex oscillating amplitudes.

nodes.name: Continuum limit of spin foams
nodes.definition: The central open problem of recovering smooth spacetime from spin foams. Recent TQFT/Refined-Algebraic-Quantisation analysis shows strong convergence forces a topological theory, motivating a distributional (rigging-map) construction of the physical Hilbert space.

nodes.name: Master constraint programme
nodes.definition: Thiemann's proposal to combine all (infinitely many) Hamiltonian constraints into a single positive 'master' constraint M = integral H^2/sqrt(det q), bypassing the non-closure of the constraint algebra with structure functions.

nodes.name: Graph-changing dynamics
nodes.definition: Dynamics in which the Hamiltonian constraint changes both spin labels and the topology of the spin-network graph. First numerically implemented in 2024, revealing solutions and observables inaccessible to fixed-graph truncations.

nodes.name: Isolated horizon
nodes.definition: A quasi-local definition of a black-hole horizon in equilibrium (no global stationarity required) carrying an SU(2) Chern-Simons theory. Horizon microstates are punctures where spin-network edges pierce the horizon.

nodes.name: SU(2) Chern-Simons horizon theory
nodes.definition: The boundary theory induced on an isolated horizon; counting its Hilbert-space dimension on a punctured sphere yields the black hole microstate count and entropy in the SU(2)-invariant formulation.

nodes.name: Black hole entropy from microstate counting
nodes.definition: LQG computes black hole entropy by counting horizon quantum-geometry microstates; matching the leading area term to S = A/4 fixes gamma, with a universal logarithmic correction -1/2 ln(A).

nodes.name: Loop quantum cosmology (LQC)
nodes.definition: Symmetry-reduced cosmological application of loop quantum gravity using Ashtekar variables, holonomies and the area gap. Replaces the Wheeler-DeWitt differential equation with a difference equation, yielding deterministic evolution across the Planck regime and a big bounce.

nodes.name: Planck star
nodes.definition: A compact high-density remnant formed when gravitational collapse bounces at Planck density inside a black hole; after evaporation a quasi-stable Planck-mass remnant may remain, proposed as a dark-matter candidate and partial resolution of the information paradox.

nodes.name: Twisted geometries / twistors
nodes.definition: Classical interpretation of the LQG phase space on a fixed graph: each edge carries two twistors, with areas and Lorentzian dihedral angles as canonical pairs; holonomies and fluxes are spinor bilinears subject to area-matching and simplicity constraints.

nodes.name: Graviton propagator from spin foams
nodes.definition: Two-point (and higher n-point) correlation functions computed from spin foam amplitudes; the low-energy limit of the improved-model two-point function matches the graviton propagator of linearized gravity in a transverse radial gauge.

nodes.name: Semiclassical limit problem
nodes.definition: The unproven recovery of general relativity as the low-energy effective theory of LQG/spin foams. Large-spin and continuum limits need not commute; graph-changing Hamiltonian dynamics complicates coherent-state analysis.

nodes.name: Deformed Lorentz symmetry / DSR
nodes.definition: Linearization of the LQG deformed hypersurface-deformation algebra connects to kappa-Poincare symmetry and Doubly Special Relativity; Lorentz invariance is deformed (with an invariant Planck scale) rather than broken.

nodes.name: Spectral triple
nodes.definition: A triple (A, H, D) of a *-algebra A represented as bounded operators on a Hilbert space H together with a self-adjoint Dirac operator D with compact resolvent such that [D, a] is bounded for all a in A. It is the noncommutative generalization of a smooth compact Riemannian spin manifold; all geometric information is encoded in D.

nodes.name: Dirac operator (spectral geometry)
nodes.definition: Self-adjoint unbounded operator D with compact resolvent that encodes the metric, dimension and differential structure of a noncommutative space; its spectrum determines the geometry and the spectral action depends only on it.

nodes.name: Real structure (charge conjugation J)
nodes.definition: Antiunitary operator J on H satisfying J^2 = epsilon, JD = epsilon' DJ, J gamma = epsilon'' gamma J, with signs fixed by the KO-dimension mod 8. It makes the spectral triple 'real', encodes charge conjugation, and the choice KO-dim 6 mod 8 resolves fermion doubling in the Standard Model.

nodes.name: KO-dimension
nodes.definition: The dimension modulo 8 (from Bott periodicity of real Clifford algebras) determining the sign table (epsilon, epsilon', epsilon'') of the real structure J. The finite Standard Model space has KO-dimension 6 mod 8; total spacetime is 4 + 6 = 10 = 2 mod 8.

nodes.name: Connes distance formula
nodes.definition: Metric reconstructed from the Dirac operator as d(phi, psi) = sup{ |phi(a) - psi(a)| : ||[D, a]|| <= 1 }. Recovers geodesic distance for pure states on a manifold and the order-1 Wasserstein (optimal transport) distance for mixed states. The metric is derived, not postulated.

nodes.name: Reconstruction theorem
nodes.definition: Connes' theorem (2008): a commutative spectral triple satisfying seven axioms (dimension, regularity, finiteness, reality, first-order, orientability, Poincare duality) necessarily arises from a compact oriented Riemannian spin manifold. Establishes the equivalence geometry = algebra + Dirac operator.

nodes.name: First-order condition
nodes.definition: Axiom [[D, a], J b* J^{-1}] = 0 stating that D acts as a first-order differential operator. Relaxing it (post-2013) yields Pati-Salam extensions and inner fluctuations beyond the Standard Model.

nodes.name: Spectral action principle
nodes.definition: Action principle S = Tr f(D/Lambda) + <psi, D psi> asserting that all physics depends only on the spectrum of the Dirac operator. Its heat-kernel expansion yields gravity (cosmological constant, Einstein-Hilbert, Weyl) plus Yang-Mills and Higgs sectors of the Standard Model from a single functional.

nodes.name: Heat-kernel (Seeley-DeWitt) expansion
nodes.definition: Asymptotic expansion Tr f(D/Lambda) ~ f_4 Lambda^4 a_0 + f_2 Lambda^2 a_2 + f_0 a_4 + O(Lambda^-2) in terms of Seeley-DeWitt coefficients a_k of D^2; produces the cosmological constant (Lambda^4), the Einstein-Hilbert term (Lambda^2, Newton constant), and the Weyl-squared term (Lambda^0).

nodes.name: Almost-commutative geometry
nodes.definition: Product of a continuous 4-manifold M with a finite noncommutative space F: A = C^infinity(M) tensor A_F. The continuous factor encodes gravity; the finite factor encodes internal gauge degrees of freedom of particle physics.

nodes.name: Finite Standard Model algebra
nodes.definition: The finite algebra A_F = C + H + M_3(C) (complex numbers, quaternions, 3x3 matrices) whose unitary group, after unimodular reduction, yields the gauge group U(1) x SU(2) x SU(3) of the Standard Model.

nodes.name: Inner fluctuations of the metric
nodes.definition: Fluctuations D -> D_A = D + A + epsilon' J A J^{-1} with A = sum a_i [D, b_i], generated by inner automorphisms of the algebra. The continuous part gives gauge fields; the discrete part gives the Higgs field.

nodes.name: Higgs field as a connection
nodes.definition: In almost-commutative geometry the Higgs field arises from off-diagonal components of the finite Dirac operator, interpreted as parallel transport in the discrete internal direction; its quartic potential is fixed by the spectral action.

nodes.name: Standard Model from spectral geometry
nodes.definition: Derivation of the full Standard Model (gauge group, fermion representations, hypercharges, Higgs, neutrino mixing) minimally coupled to gravity from the spectral action on an almost-commutative geometry with finite algebra C + H + M_3(C).

nodes.name: Fermion doubling problem
nodes.definition: Spurious multiplication of fermionic degrees of freedom in the noncommutative Standard Model, resolved by choosing KO-dimension 6 mod 8 for the finite space and using the real structure J to enforce correct charge-conjugate pairing.

nodes.name: Quanta of geometry (volume quantization)
nodes.definition: A higher-degree Heisenberg relation involving D and Feynman-slashed coordinates Y (Y^2=1) implies quantization of the volume in Planck units; in dimension 4 the two-sided version forces the algebras M_2(H) and M_4(C) underlying the Standard Model.

nodes.name: Noncommutative spacetime
nodes.definition: Spacetime whose coordinates are operators with nonzero commutators: canonical/Moyal [x^mu, x^nu] = i theta^{mu nu} or Lie-algebra type kappa-Minkowski [x^0, x^i] = (i/kappa) x^i, implying minimal length and modified dispersion relations.

nodes.name: Moyal star product
nodes.definition: Noncommutative product (f star g)(x) = exp((i/2) theta^{mu nu} d_mu^x d_nu^y) f(x) g(y)|_{y=x} replacing pointwise multiplication; encodes canonical noncommutativity at the level of the function algebra. Appears in D-brane effective theories.

nodes.name: kappa-Minkowski spacetime
nodes.definition: Lie-algebra noncommutative spacetime with [x^0, x^i] = (i/kappa) x^i, [x^i, x^j] = 0, where kappa is a Planck-energy scale. It is dual to the momentum sector of the kappa-Poincare Hopf algebra and underlies many DSR models.

nodes.name: kappa-Poincare Hopf algebra
nodes.definition: Quantum (Hopf-algebra) deformation of the Poincare algebra that is the symmetry of kappa-Minkowski spacetime; its bicrossproduct basis yields deformed boosts and an energy-dependent speed of light, reducing to ordinary Poincare as kappa -> infinity.

nodes.name: Doubly/deformed special relativity (DSR)
nodes.definition: Relativistic framework with two observer-independent scales: the speed of light c and a Planck length/energy scale. Realized via kappa-Poincare; predicts deformed dispersion relations and energy-dependent photon velocity testable with gamma-ray bursts.

nodes.name: Doplicher-Fredenhagen-Roberts quantum spacetime
nodes.definition: Model deriving spacetime noncommutativity [x^mu, x^nu] = i ell_P^2 Q^{mu nu} from the requirement that localizing an event better than the Planck length would create a black hole, combining the Heisenberg principle with classical gravity.

nodes.name: Fuzzy spaces
nodes.definition: Finite matrix approximations of manifolds (e.g. fuzzy sphere S^2_N) whose function algebra is M_N(C); spherical harmonics are truncated at a maximal spin. They are finite spectral triples with matrix Dirac operators and converge to the continuum as N -> infinity.

nodes.name: Fuzzy sphere
nodes.definition: Canonical noncommutative geometry approximating S^2: algebra M_N(C) generated by truncated spherical harmonics with [x_i, x_j] = i (theta) epsilon_{ijk} x_k; recovers the round 2-sphere as N -> infinity.

nodes.name: Random noncommutative geometry (Dirac ensembles)
nodes.definition: Path-integral quantization over fluctuating finite Dirac operators on finite real spectral triples (fuzzy geometries), a noncommutative analogue of integration over metrics. Reduces to multimatrix integrals; studied by Barrett-Glaser numerically and analytically.

nodes.name: Matrix models (IKKT/BFSS, multimatrix)
nodes.definition: Models whose dynamical variables are large matrices; fuzzy spaces and noncommutative gauge theories emerge from them, and effective Riemannian geometry/gravity emerges from matrix fluctuations. Multimatrix integrals arise from Dirac ensembles.

nodes.name: Spectral Pati-Salam model
nodes.definition: Beyond-Standard-Model unification SU(2)_L x SU(2)_R x SU(4) obtained by relaxing the first-order condition in the spectral framework, exhibiting grand unification of the Pati-Salam gauge couplings.

nodes.name: Seiberg-Witten map
nodes.definition: Explicit field redefinition mapping a commutative gauge theory to an equivalent noncommutative (Moyal) one; underlies the equivalence of commutative and noncommutative descriptions of D-branes in a constant B-field.

nodes.name: Noncommutative quantum field theory
nodes.definition: QFT on noncommutative spacetime (Moyal or kappa-Minkowski) using star products; exhibits UV/IR mixing, modified propagators and, for kappa-Minkowski, nontrivial coproducts requiring braided/Hopf-algebraic treatment.

nodes.name: Wave function of the universe
nodes.definition: A quantum state Psi encoding the entire cosmology, defined either as a solution of the Wheeler-DeWitt equation or via a gravitational path integral with specified boundary conditions. Its modulus squared is interpreted (problematically) as a probability for a given 3-geometry.

nodes.name: Hartle-Hawking no-boundary proposal
nodes.definition: Proposal that the wave function of the universe is given by a Euclidean path integral over compact 4-geometries whose only boundary is the prescribed 3-geometry, so spacetime closes off smoothly with no initial boundary (the shuttlecock picture). Weights small cosmological constant more heavily.

nodes.name: Vilenkin tunneling proposal
nodes.definition: Proposal that the universe is created by quantum tunneling from nothing (no classical spacetime, matter or energy) into a de Sitter phase; the wave function contains only outgoing (expanding) waves asymptotically. Weights large cosmological constant, favoring inflation.

nodes.name: Euclidean gravitational path integral
nodes.definition: Path integral over Euclidean-signature 4-geometries weighted by exp(-I_E/hbar); used to define the no-boundary wave function and, more generally, the partition function and entropy in gravity. Conditionally convergent and requires contour prescription.

nodes.name: Lorentzian gravitational path integral
nodes.definition: Path integral over Lorentzian-signature geometries weighted by exp(iS/hbar); for minisuperspace it is rendered absolutely convergent by Picard-Lefschetz theory. Central to the Feldbrugge-Lehners-Turok reformulation of the no-boundary and tunneling proposals.

nodes.name: Picard-Lefschetz theory
nodes.definition: Mathematical framework that deforms an oscillatory integration contour onto steepest-descent paths (Lefschetz thimbles), turning a conditionally convergent path integral into an absolutely convergent one and identifying the relevant complex saddle points.

nodes.name: Kontsevich-Segal allowability criterion
nodes.definition: Criterion specifying which complex metrics are physically admissible, by requiring that quantum field theories can be consistently defined on them (sum of degrees of rotation of the metric eigenvalues bounded by pi). Used to constrain saddles in quantum cosmology and inflation.

nodes.name: Holonomy and inverse-volume corrections
nodes.definition: Two classes of quantum corrections in LQC. Holonomy corrections (from representing the connection by holonomies around small loops, mu-bar / improved-dynamics scheme) dominate the bounce; inverse-volume corrections dominate at small volumes.

nodes.name: Area gap
nodes.definition: Smallest nonzero eigenvalue of the area operator in loop quantum gravity, Delta = 4 sqrt(3) pi gamma l_Planck^2, with gamma the Barbero-Immirzi parameter (~0.2375). Sets the discreteness scale that regularizes the bounce and fixes the critical density.

nodes.name: Critical (bounce) density
nodes.definition: Maximum energy density rho_c = sqrt(3)/(32 pi^2 gamma^3 G^2 hbar) approximately 0.41 rho_Planck at which the LQC big bounce occurs; a sharp quantitative prediction of loop quantum cosmology.

nodes.name: Trans-Planckian problem
nodes.definition: In the standard derivation of Hawking radiation, the emitted modes traced back to the horizon have arbitrarily high (trans-Planckian) frequencies, though these do not appear in the final answer. Raises the question of whether the result is robust against unknown UV/Planck-scale physics. Robust for monotonic dispersion and invariant cutoffs; modified by non-monotonic dispersion after the scrambling time.

nodes.name: Trans-Planckian Censorship Conjecture (TCC)
nodes.definition: Sub-Planckian quantum fluctuations must never be stretched by cosmic expansion beyond the Hubble horizon and freeze: (a_f/a_i) l_Pl < 1/H_f. Over a monotonic stretch this implies |grad V|/V >= 2/sqrt((d-1)(d-2)) / M_Pl, sharpening asymptotically to |grad V|/V >= 2/sqrt(d-2) / M_Pl, forbidding eternal acceleration. Bounds inflation scale to V^{1/4} <~ 10^9 GeV and epsilon < 10^-31 (Bedroya-Vafa 2019).

nodes.name: Eternal inflation
nodes.definition: Regime in which quantum fluctuations of the inflaton keep inflation going forever in an ever-growing comoving volume, with reheating completing only in disconnected pocket universes, generically producing a multiverse.

nodes.name: Measure problem
nodes.definition: The problem of assigning well-defined probabilities in an eternally inflating multiverse where volumes and observer counts diverge; no canonical normalization exists, and proposed measures (causal patch, scale-factor cutoff, stationary) suffer paradoxes (Boltzmann brains, youngness).

nodes.name: Multiverse
nodes.definition: Ensemble of causally disconnected pocket universes with potentially different effective constants and vacua, produced by eternal inflation populating the string landscape; the framework in which the measure problem and anthropic reasoning arise.

nodes.name: Ekpyrotic / cyclic cosmology
nodes.definition: Alternative to inflation in which a slowly contracting phase with ultra-stiff equation of state w >> 1 generates a nearly scale-invariant spectrum via the entropic (two-field) mechanism, predicting negligible tensor modes (r ~ 0) and local non-Gaussianity f_NL ~ 5; cyclic versions iterate cosmic epochs.

nodes.name: String gas cosmology
nodes.definition: Early-universe model with a quasi-static Hagedorn phase of a gas of highly excited strings; T-duality and winding modes resolve the initial singularity and generate a nearly scale-invariant spectrum with a characteristic slightly blue tensor tilt.

nodes.name: Pre-big-bang scenario
nodes.definition: String-cosmology scenario (Gasperini-Veneziano) with a dilaton-driven super-inflationary phase preceding the big bang, motivated by scale-factor (T/S) duality of string theory.

nodes.name: String cosmology
nodes.definition: Application of string theory to the early universe, encompassing string gas, pre-big-bang, ekpyrotic/cyclic, double field theory and matrix cosmology; uses dualities, the dilaton, branes and the Hagedorn temperature.

nodes.name: Quantum origin of structure
nodes.definition: The hypothesis that all cosmic structure originated from primordial quantum fluctuations of the inflaton/metric, amplified by expansion and converted (via squeezing and decoherence) into classical density perturbations imprinted on the CMB.

nodes.name: Cosmic decoherence / quantum-to-classical transition
nodes.definition: Mechanism by which highly squeezed superhorizon perturbation modes become effectively classical: tracing out environmental/short-wavelength degrees of freedom diagonalizes the reduced density matrix in the field-amplitude (pointer) basis, yielding stochastic classical inhomogeneities and an emergent arrow of time.

nodes.name: Mukhanov-Sasaki perturbation spectrum
nodes.definition: Common framework for primordial perturbations: v_k'' + (k^2 - z''/z) v_k = 0 with curvature power spectrum P_R = (k^3/2pi^2)|v_k/z|^2; inflation, LQC, ekpyrotic and string gas scenarios differ in the background pump z''/z and vacuum choice.

nodes.name: Holographic cosmology
nodes.definition: Reformulation of the no-boundary measure and eternal inflation via AdS/CFT and dS/CFT: complex no-boundary saddles have a dual description as deformed Euclidean CFT partition functions on a final boundary, suggesting a finite, smooth multiverse (Hawking-Hertog smooth exit).

nodes.name: Dark energy from quantum gravity
nodes.definition: Attempts to derive or constrain dark energy from quantum-gravity principles: swampland de Sitter / TCC conjectures favoring dynamical quintessence over a constant Lambda, causal-set prediction of Lambda ~ 1/sqrt(V) fluctuations, and the DESI 2024-2025 hint of an evolving equation of state.

nodes.name: QFT in Curved Spacetime (QFTCS)
nodes.definition: Quantum field theory of matter fields propagating on a fixed classical globally hyperbolic spacetime (M,g). Gravity is treated classically and unquantized; there is no preferred vacuum and no observer-independent notion of particle. The foundational regime in which Unruh, Hawking and cosmological particle-production effects arise.

nodes.name: Bogoliubov Transformation
nodes.definition: Linear transformation relating two complete mode bases of a quantum field, with coefficients alpha (mixing positive frequencies) and beta (mixing positive and negative frequencies). Nonzero beta means the 'in' vacuum contains 'out' particles; the particle number is sum |beta_ji|^2. The universal mechanism behind particle creation in Unruh, Hawking and cosmological contexts.

nodes.name: Unruh-DeWitt Detector
nodes.definition: An idealized pointlike two-level system linearly coupled to the quantum field; its response (excitation rate) is the Fourier transform of the field's Wightman function pulled back along the detector trajectory. Operationalizes the otherwise observer-dependent notion of a 'particle' as a detector click.

nodes.name: KMS Condition
nodes.definition: The Kubo-Martin-Schwinger analyticity condition <A sigma_t(B)> = <sigma_{t-i beta}(B) A> characterizing thermal equilibrium of a state with respect to a flow at inverse temperature beta. Tomita-Takesaki shows every cyclic-separating state is KMS at beta=1 with respect to its own modular flow: equilibrium and modular time are two views of the same structure. Identifies the restriction of the Minkowski vacuum to a wedge as a thermal state (Unruh).

nodes.name: Cosmological / Gravitational Particle Production (GPP)
nodes.definition: Creation of particles by a time-dependent FLRW metric, which mixes positive and negative frequencies between adiabatic-vacuum in/out states. Conformally coupled massless fields are not produced in FLRW (conformal triviality); massive and minimally coupled fields are. A generic source of dark matter, dark radiation and cosmological relics during inflation and reheating.

nodes.name: Adiabatic Vacuum
nodes.definition: An approximate vacuum state defined in slowly varying (adiabatic) cosmological backgrounds via a WKB expansion of mode functions; provides a physically motivated notion of 'no particles' and an order-by-order regularization (adiabatic subtraction) of the stress-energy tensor in FLRW.

nodes.name: Hadamard State
nodes.definition: A physically admissible state whose two-point function has the same short-distance singular structure as the Minkowski vacuum (Hadamard parametrix). Equivalently (Radzikowski) it satisfies the microlocal spectrum condition on its wavefront set. Ensures finiteness of the renormalized stress-energy tensor and serves as the curved-space replacement for a vacuum.

nodes.name: Microlocal Spectrum Condition
nodes.definition: A constraint on the wavefront set of the n-point distributions of a state, requiring singularities to propagate only along future-directed null geodesics (microlocal positive-frequency). Radzikowski proved its equivalence to the geometric Hadamard form, replacing the global spectrum condition unavailable in generic curved spacetime.

nodes.name: Renormalized Stress-Energy Tensor
nodes.definition: The expectation value <T_ab> obtained by subtracting the universal Hadamard parametrix (point-splitting/Hadamard renormalization). Satisfies Wald's axioms (local covariance, causality, conservation). For massless fields the ambiguity is two-parameter; for massive fields it is infinite-parameter (local curvature counterterms). Sources the semiclassical Einstein equation.

nodes.name: Wald Axioms for the Stress-Energy Tensor
nodes.definition: A set of axioms (local covariance, conservation, causality/locality, agreement with normal-ordering in Minkowski) that any acceptable renormalized <T_ab> must satisfy. They determine <T_ab> up to local curvature terms, fixing the renormalization ambiguity to a finite (massless) or infinite (massive) number of parameters.

nodes.name: Trace (Conformal) Anomaly
nodes.definition: The quantum non-vanishing of the trace of the stress tensor for a classically conformal field: <T^a_a> = (1/(4pi)^2)(c C_abcd C^abcd - a E_4 + b box R), with C the Weyl tensor and E_4 the Euler/Gauss-Bonnet density. The a,c coefficients are model dependent. Fixes <T_ab> in conformally flat spacetimes and gives an anomaly route to Hawking flux.

nodes.name: Semiclassical Einstein Equation
nodes.definition: G_ab + Lambda g_ab = 8 pi G_N (<T_ab>_omega + alpha H1_ab + beta H2_ab): the classical metric is sourced by the renormalized expectation value of the quantum stress tensor, with higher-derivative counterterms. The system is fourth order (16 free data vs 4 constraints), produces runaway solutions, and requires a Hadamard state on the metric it itself generates.

nodes.name: Order Reduction (Parker-Simon)
nodes.definition: A prescription to tame the fourth-order semiclassical Einstein equation by perturbatively re-expressing higher derivatives via the lower-order equations, reducing it to an effective second-order system and removing spurious runaway (Horowitz) solutions that grow on the Planck timescale.

nodes.name: Validity Criterion of Semiclassical Gravity
nodes.definition: The question of when replacing T_ab by <T_ab> is justified. A stability/fluctuation criterion (via the noise kernel and Einstein-Langevin equation, Hu-Verdaguer) requires induced metric fluctuations to remain small; semiclassicality is expected to fail near the Page time and in the near-horizon region of an evaporating black hole, and for macroscopic 'cat states'.

nodes.name: Noise Kernel
nodes.definition: The symmetrized two-point connected correlation function of the stress-tensor fluctuation operator, N_abcd(x,x') = (1/2)<{t_ab(x), t_cd(x')}>, which acts as the source covariance of the stochastic force in the Einstein-Langevin equation. Its magnitude provides a quantitative validity criterion for semiclassical gravity.

nodes.name: Algebraic / Locally Covariant QFT
nodes.definition: Formulation of QFTCS in terms of *-algebras of observables on globally hyperbolic spacetimes, with locally covariant QFT realized as a functor from the category of such spacetimes (with causal isometric embeddings) to the category of unital algebras. Encodes general covariance and causality without a preferred vacuum.

nodes.name: Tomita-Takesaki Modular Theory
nodes.definition: Operator-algebraic framework associating a modular Hamiltonian and modular flow to a state on a von Neumann algebra of a spacetime region. In QFTCS it underlies the geometric action of boosts on the Rindler wedge (Bisognano-Wichmann), the definition of relative entropy, and rigorous proofs of energy conditions.

nodes.name: Relative Entropy in QFT
nodes.definition: Araki's relative entropy S(omega||vacuum) between a state and the vacuum, well-defined in QFT via modular theory. Equals modular energy minus vacuum-subtracted entanglement entropy; its positivity and monotonicity yield rigorous, model-independent proofs of the Bekenstein bound and the quantum null energy condition.

nodes.name: Quantum Energy Inequalities (QEI)
nodes.definition: State-independent lower bounds on weighted time-averages of the renormalized energy density (or null-projected stress tensor) of a quantum field, preventing arbitrarily large or persistent negative energy. The basis for proving ANEC, QNEC, and semiclassical singularity theorems in curved spacetime.

nodes.name: Averaged Null Energy Condition (ANEC)
nodes.definition: The condition that the integral of T_ab k^a k^b along a complete achronal null geodesic is non-negative. Violated pointwise by quantum fields but provable as an averaged statement from null-projected quantum inequalities; used to forbid traversable wormholes, superluminal travel and closed timelike curves.

nodes.name: Semiclassical Singularity Theorem
nodes.definition: A Penrose-Hawking-type singularity theorem (geodesic incompleteness) derived not from a pointwise energy condition but from a quantum energy inequality satisfied by the quantized field (Fewster-Kontou 2022). Brings singularity theorems within the scope of QFT, predicting incompleteness on physically plausible timescales.

nodes.name: Wald Entropy
nodes.definition: Black-hole entropy expressed as the Noether charge of diffeomorphism invariance: S_W = -2 pi integral over the horizon cross-section of (dL/dR_abcd) eps_ab eps_cd. For the Einstein-Hilbert Lagrangian it reduces to A/4G; for higher-curvature (Lovelock, Gauss-Bonnet, f(R)) theories it gives the correct corrections and satisfies the first law.

nodes.name: Gravitationally Induced Decoherence
nodes.definition: Loss of quantum coherence of spatial superpositions of matter due to coupling to the gravitational field (quantized or classical/stochastic). Includes Diosi-Penrose decoherence and decoherence by emission of soft gravitons; a key potentially observable discriminator between semiclassical and quantized-gravity scenarios.

nodes.name: Spacetime Superposition
nodes.definition: A quantum superposition of distinct gravitational fields / spacetime geometries sourced by a mass in superposition of locations. Central to Penrose's collapse argument and to debates over whether GIE experiments genuinely probe quantized geometry or only the Newtonian potential.

nodes.name: Fundamental string
nodes.definition: One-dimensional extended relativistic object whose motion sweeps out a two-dimensional worldsheet. Open strings have endpoints; closed strings are loops. Characteristic length scale is the string length l_s = sqrt(alpha'), with tension T = 1/(2 pi alpha'). Vibrational modes correspond to particles, including a massless spin-2 graviton in the closed-string spectrum.

nodes.name: Worldsheet conformal field theory
nodes.definition: The 2D quantum field theory living on the string worldsheet, governed by the Polyakov action. Reparametrization plus Weyl invariance make it a conformal field theory whose Virasoro algebra has a central charge. Consistency (vanishing total central charge including ghosts) fixes the spacetime dimension and forces the target-space Einstein equations as the beta-function vanishing condition.

nodes.name: Critical dimension
nodes.definition: The number of spacetime dimensions for which the conformal (Weyl) anomaly on the worldsheet cancels: D=26 for the bosonic string and D=10 for the superstring. The anomaly is proportional to (D-26) for the bosonic case (each X contributes c=1, the bc ghosts contribute c=-26) and to (3/2)(D-10) for the superstring.

nodes.name: Conformal (Weyl) anomaly
nodes.definition: Quantum breaking of worldsheet Weyl invariance; its coefficient is the total Virasoro central charge. Requiring it to vanish (c_total = 0) selects the critical dimension and, in curved backgrounds, requires the spacetime beta functions to vanish, which are precisely the (super)gravity equations of motion.

nodes.name: Bosonic string theory
nodes.definition: Simplest string theory containing only bosonic worldsheet fields. Lives in D=26, contains a tachyon in its ground state (M^2 = -1/alpha' for the open string, signaling vacuum instability) and no spacetime fermions. Mainly a pedagogical and structural laboratory; not a viable theory of nature.

nodes.name: Superstring theory
nodes.definition: String theory with worldsheet supersymmetry (added fermionic fields). Lives in D=10, is tachyon-free after the GSO projection, and contains spacetime fermions and supersymmetry. Comes in five consistent versions: Type I, Type IIA, Type IIB, heterotic SO(32), heterotic E8xE8.

nodes.name: GSO projection
nodes.definition: Gliozzi-Scherk-Olive truncation of the superstring spectrum keeping states of definite worldsheet fermion number (-1)^F. It removes the tachyon, yields a spacetime-supersymmetric and modular-invariant spectrum, and is essential for the consistency of the superstring.

nodes.name: Five superstring theories
nodes.definition: The five consistent 10D superstring theories: Type I (open+closed, gauge group SO(32), N=1), Type IIA (closed, non-chiral, N=2), Type IIB (closed, chiral, N=2), heterotic SO(32) and heterotic E8xE8 (hybrid of bosonic left-movers and superstring right-movers). All are connected by T/S/U dualities and unified by M-theory.

nodes.name: Heterotic string
nodes.definition: String whose left-movers are bosonic (effectively in D=26 with 16 internal dimensions compactified on an even self-dual lattice) and right-movers are superstring (D=10). Consistency forces the gauge group to be SO(32) or E8xE8; the E8xE8 case contains the Standard Model gauge group and was the leading phenomenological candidate (Gross-Harvey-Martinec-Rohm 1985).

nodes.name: Green-Schwarz anomaly cancellation
nodes.definition: Mechanism (Green-Schwarz 1984) by which gauge and gravitational anomalies of 10D N=1 supergravity coupled to super-Yang-Mills cancel only for gauge groups SO(32) and E8xE8, via a B-field counterterm B ^ X_8. Triggered the first superstring revolution by isolating consistent gauge theories.

nodes.name: D-brane
nodes.definition: A (p+1)-dimensional hypersurface (Dp-brane) on which open strings end (Dirichlet boundary conditions). D-branes carry Ramond-Ramond charge, are dynamical, have tension scaling as 1/g_s, and host a gauge theory on their worldvolume. They are essential for string dualities, supply the microstates in black-hole entropy counting, and underlie AdS/CFT (Polchinski 1995).

nodes.name: Ramond-Ramond charge
nodes.definition: Charge under the antisymmetric p-form gauge potentials in the Ramond-Ramond sector of Type II strings. D-branes are the sources of RR charge, with charges quantized by a Dirac condition exactly as required by string duality, which is why D-branes are intrinsic to Type II string theory.

nodes.name: String dualities (T/S/U)
nodes.definition: Web of equivalences relating the string theories. T-duality maps a string on a circle of radius R to radius alpha'/R (exchanging momentum and winding modes), relating IIA<->IIB and heterotic SO(32)<->E8xE8. S-duality exchanges strong and weak coupling g_s <-> 1/g_s (e.g. Type I <-> heterotic SO(32), IIB self-dual). U-duality unifies T and S, realized by exceptional groups E_n(n).

nodes.name: T-duality
nodes.definition: Target-space duality identifying string compactified on a circle of radius R with one on radius alpha'/R, exchanging Kaluza-Klein momentum number n with winding number m. The self-dual radius is R = sqrt(alpha'). A genuinely stringy symmetry with no point-particle analogue.

nodes.name: S-duality
nodes.definition: Strong-weak coupling duality g_s <-> 1/g_s that exchanges fundamental strings with solitonic (D- or NS5-brane) objects. Relates Type I to heterotic SO(32), maps IIB to itself, and is non-perturbative evidence for the unity of string theory.

nodes.name: U-duality
nodes.definition: Unification of T- and S-dualities in toroidally compactified string/M-theory, realized by the exceptional groups E_n(n)(Z). It acts on the full BPS spectrum and the moduli space, and is a central organizing principle of M-theory (Obers-Pioline 1999, Hull-Townsend).

nodes.name: M-theory
nodes.definition: Conjectured 11-dimensional quantum theory whose low-energy limit is 11D supergravity and whose various limits/compactifications give the five superstring theories. Strongly coupled Type IIA grows an 11th dimension of radius R_11 = g_s^(2/3) l_p; M-theory has no fundamental string but M2- and M5-branes. A complete non-perturbative definition remains unknown (Witten 1995).

nodes.name: 11D supergravity
nodes.definition: Unique maximal supergravity in 11 dimensions (Cremmer-Julia-Scherk 1978), with metric, gravitino and a 3-form potential C_3 (sourcing M2-branes) whose 7-form dual sources M5-branes. It is the low-energy effective field theory of M-theory.

nodes.name: Compactification
nodes.definition: Reduction of the 10D/11D theory to 4D by curling up extra dimensions on a compact internal manifold. Preserving N=1 supersymmetry in 4D requires the internal space to be a Calabi-Yau threefold (SU(3) holonomy). The geometry of the internal manifold determines the 4D gauge group, matter content and couplings.

nodes.name: Calabi-Yau compactification
nodes.definition: Compactification on a Calabi-Yau threefold (Ricci-flat, SU(3) holonomy Kahler manifold) preserving minimal N=1 supersymmetry in 4D. The topology (Hodge numbers, intersection numbers) fixes the number of generations and moduli. Introduces a vast number of geometric choices feeding the landscape.

nodes.name: Moduli stabilization
nodes.definition: Fixing the massless scalar moduli (shape/size of the internal manifold, dilaton) by generating a potential, otherwise they yield unobserved long-range forces and undetermined couplings. Achieved in scenarios like KKLT (fluxes fix complex-structure moduli and dilaton; non-perturbative effects fix Kahler moduli) and the Large Volume Scenario.

nodes.name: Flux compactification
nodes.definition: Compactification with nonzero background values (fluxes) of p-form field strengths threading internal cycles. Fluxes generate a superpotential that lifts and stabilizes moduli, and their quantized choices produce an enormous discretuum of vacua (the landscape).

nodes.name: String landscape
nodes.definition: The vast set of consistent low-energy effective field theories (EFTs) that arise as low-energy limits of string theory, typically from flux compactifications of Calabi-Yau / F-theory backgrounds. Statistical estimates range from ~10^500 to lower bounds of 10^272000 flux vacua. The multiplicity motivates anthropic selection of the cosmological constant and Standard Model parameters.

nodes.name: de Sitter vacua
nodes.definition: String vacua with a positive cosmological constant, needed to model the observed dark energy. Constructed via uplift (e.g. anti-D3 branes in KKLT), but their full control is debated; the refined de Sitter swampland conjecture claims metastable dS may be impossible, favoring quintessence instead.

nodes.name: Swampland Distance Conjecture
nodes.definition: Conjecture that traversing a super-Planckian geodesic distance Delta in moduli space triggers an infinite tower of states whose mass falls exponentially, m ~ m_0 exp(-alpha Delta/M_Pl), causing the effective field theory to break down. One of the best-supported swampland constraints.

nodes.name: Weak Gravity Conjecture
nodes.definition: In any consistent quantum gravity with a U(1) gauge field of coupling g there must exist a charged particle for which the gauge force is at least as strong as gravity: q g M_Pl >= m, i.e. its charge-to-mass ratio exceeds that of an extremal black hole. Ensures extremal black holes can decay. Variants: electric, magnetic (cutoff Lambda <~ g M_Pl), convex-hull (multiple U(1)s), lattice, sublattice/tower.

nodes.name: Emergent String Conjecture
nodes.definition: Refinement of the SDC: every infinite-distance limit in moduli space is either a decompactification limit (a KK tower becomes light, dimension grows) or a limit in which a unique critical, asymptotically tensionless, weakly coupled string becomes the lightest object. Established for M-/IIA on CY3 by Lee-Lerche-Weigand (2019).

nodes.name: Species scale
nodes.definition: Gravitational cutoff in the presence of N light species: Lambda_sp ~ M_Pl / sqrt(N), below the Planck scale. Marks where EFT description of gravity breaks down; equals the inverse size of the smallest black hole whose Bekenstein-Hawking entropy S_BH ~ N. Central to the emergence proposal and the universal pattern.

nodes.name: Strominger-Vafa black-hole entropy
nodes.definition: First microscopic derivation of the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy (1996): the degeneracy of BPS bound states of Q1 D1-branes, Q5 D5-branes and n momentum units in Type IIB reproduces S = 2 pi sqrt(Q1 Q5 n) = A/(4G) exactly, via the Cardy formula of the dual 2D CFT with central charge c = 6 Q1 Q5.

nodes.name: Cardy formula
nodes.definition: Universal asymptotic growth of the density of states of a 2D CFT with central charge c: S = 2 pi sqrt((c/6)(L_0 - c/24)) (plus the right-moving copy). It underlies microscopic black-hole entropy counting and the universality of near-horizon (AdS3) microstate counting.

nodes.name: BPS states
nodes.definition: States saturating a Bogomolnyi-Prasad-Sommerfield bound (mass equal to central charge), preserving a fraction of supersymmetry. Their degeneracies are protected against continuation in the coupling, which is what makes weak-coupling D-brane counting valid at strong coupling where the black hole forms.

nodes.name: AdS/CFT correspondence
nodes.definition: Maldacena's holographic duality (1997) equating Type IIB string theory on AdS5 x S5 with 4D N=4 super-Yang-Mills, derived from the two descriptions of D3-branes. A strong-weak duality and the sharpest realization of the holographic principle; provides a non-perturbative definition of quantum gravity in asymptotically AdS space.

nodes.name: BFSS matrix model
nodes.definition: Maximally supersymmetric U(N) matrix quantum mechanics (Banks-Fischler-Shenker-Susskind 1996) describing N D0-branes, conjectured to give a non-perturbative definition of M-theory in the infinite-momentum/DLCQ frame. Spacetime coordinates become non-commuting matrices; gravity and branes emerge from matrix dynamics.

nodes.name: IKKT matrix model
nodes.definition: Type IIB (0+0)-dimensional matrix model (Ishibashi-Kawai-Kitazawa-Tsuchiya 1997), the dimensional reduction of 10D super-Yang-Mills to a point. Proposed as a non-perturbative, manifestly Lorentz-covariant definition of Type IIB superstring; candidate for dynamical emergence of (3+1)-dimensional spacetime.

nodes.name: Hagedorn temperature
nodes.definition: Limiting temperature T_H ~ 1/l_s at which the canonical partition function of a string gas diverges because the density of states grows exponentially, rho(M) ~ M^(-a) exp(beta_H M). Signals a phase transition / breakdown of standard thermodynamics; relevant to early-universe string cosmology and black-hole/string transitions.

nodes.name: String thermodynamics
nodes.definition: Statistical mechanics of strings, dominated by the exponential Hagedorn density of states. Underlies the string-black-hole correspondence (a highly excited long string has the same entropy scaling as a black hole at the correspondence point) and string-gas cosmology.

nodes.name: String field theory
nodes.definition: Second-quantized formulation of string theory with a spacetime action functional of a string field, off-shell and (in principle) non-perturbative. Witten's cubic open bosonic SFT (Chern-Simons-like action) admits analytic tachyon-vacuum solutions (Schnabl, Erler-Schnabl); closed SFT requires an L-infinity structure with infinitely many vertices.

nodes.name: Tachyon
nodes.definition: A state with negative mass-squared, signaling instability of the vacuum. Present in the bosonic string and on unstable D-brane systems; removed in the superstring by the GSO projection. Open-string tachyon condensation describes D-brane decay (Sen's conjectures, solved analytically in cubic SFT).

nodes.name: Regge trajectory
nodes.definition: Linear relation J <= alpha' M^2 + const between angular momentum and mass-squared of string excitations, with slope alpha' (the Regge slope). It was the original 1960s motivation (Veneziano amplitude, dual resonance model) before strings were reinterpreted as quantum gravity.

nodes.name: String tension / Regge slope
nodes.definition: The energy per unit length of the string, T = 1/(2 pi alpha'), setting the fundamental scale. alpha' is the Regge slope and l_s = sqrt(alpha') the string length; in superstring phenomenology this scale is typically near the Planck scale.

nodes.name: String coupling
nodes.definition: Dimensionless coupling g_s = exp(<Phi>) governing the strength of string interactions, set by the vacuum expectation value of the dilaton Phi. D-brane tension scales as 1/g_s and NS5-brane tension as 1/g_s^2; S-duality acts as g_s <-> 1/g_s.

nodes.name: Low-energy effective (super)gravity action
nodes.definition: The spacetime effective action obtained by integrating out massive string modes; its leading terms are 10D supergravity (plus alpha' and g_s corrections). Einstein's equations appear as the condition of worldsheet conformal invariance (vanishing beta functions), not as an input postulate.

nodes.name: Perturbative non-renormalizability of gravity
nodes.definition: Because Newton's constant has negative mass dimension ([G]=M^-2), the superficial degree of divergence grows with loop order (D=2L+2 in d=4), requiring infinitely many independent counterterms of ever higher curvature dimension and destroying predictivity in the UV.

nodes.name: Goroff-Sagnotti two-loop counterterm
nodes.definition: Nonzero two-loop divergence of pure Einstein gravity proportional to the cube of the Weyl/Riemann tensor (C^3) with coefficient 209/2880; it cannot be removed by field redefinition or equations of motion, definitively proving perturbative non-renormalizability.

nodes.name: Gravity as an effective field theory (Donoghue)
nodes.definition: General relativity treated as a low-energy EFT: unknown UV physics is absorbed into renormalized coefficients of a local Lagrangian, while nonanalytic terms (ln, sqrt of momentum) give universal, parameter-independent low-energy quantum predictions such as the leading correction to the Newtonian potential.

nodes.name: Nonanalytic quantum corrections
nodes.definition: Parts of loop amplitudes that are nonanalytic in momentum transfer (e.g. sqrt(-q^2), ln(-q^2)); they dominate at long distance, are insensitive to UV counterterms, and yield reliable, UV-completion-independent predictions in the EFT of gravity.

nodes.name: Quantum correction to the Newtonian potential
nodes.definition: Leading genuine quantum (hbar) correction to V(r) computed in the EFT of gravity, with universal coefficient 41/(10 pi); accompanied by the classical post-Newtonian 3G(m1+m2)/(rc^2) term. Too small to measure but a mandatory IR check for any quantum gravity theory.

nodes.name: Supergravity (N=1..8)
nodes.definition: Locally supersymmetric extension of general relativity with N supercharges and a gravitino (spin 3/2). Higher N gives stronger UV cancellations; N=8 is maximal in 4D since more supersymmetry would force spins greater than 2.

nodes.name: N=8 supergravity
nodes.definition: Maximal 4D supergravity (toroidal reduction of 11D supergravity) with 1 graviton, 8 gravitinos, 28 vectors, 56 fermions, 70 scalars; hidden global E7(7) and local SU(8) symmetry. Candidate for a perturbatively UV-finite point-particle quantum gravity.

nodes.name: E7(7) duality symmetry
nodes.definition: Exceptional continuous global symmetry of N=8 supergravity that constrains allowed UV counterterms; F-term counterterms cannot be E7(7)-invariant below seven loops, so the lowest admissible invariant counterterm (partial^6 R^4) appears only at L=7.

nodes.name: Enhanced UV cancellations
nodes.definition: UV cancellations in supergravity amplitudes that go beyond what all known symmetries and superspace power-counting predict (e.g. N=5 finite at 4 loops, N=8 better than expected at 3-5 loops). No symmetry-based explanation currently exists.

nodes.name: Critical dimension D_c(L) for N=8 UV finiteness
nodes.definition: Spacetime dimension below which the L-loop four-point amplitude of N=8 supergravity is finite: D_c=6 (3 loops), 11/2 (4 loops), 24/5 (5 loops). The relation D_c=4+6/L (the N=4 SYM bound) holds only through four loops; at five loops D_c=24/5 lies below the 26/5 that formula would give, but Bern et al. (2018) show 24/5 is exactly the standard D^8R^4 / E7(7) prediction (no enhanced cancellation at five loops). D=4 is reached at L=7, making the seventh loop critical for the 4D finiteness question.

nodes.name: N=5 supergravity four-loop finiteness
nodes.definition: N=5 supergravity is UV finite at four loops despite supersymmetry and duality arguments predicting a divergence; the cancellation is an enhanced cancellation, contrasting with N=4 which diverges at four loops via a U(1) duality anomaly.

nodes.name: Double copy / KLT / BCJ color-kinematics duality
nodes.definition: The statement gravity = (gauge theory)^2. When color factors satisfy Jacobi identities, kinematic numerators can be chosen to satisfy the same identities (BCJ duality); replacing a color factor by a second copy of the kinematic numerator turns a gauge amplitude into a gravity amplitude. The field-theory limit of KLT relations; extends (proven in cases, conjectured generally) to loop level.

nodes.name: Generalized unitarity method
nodes.definition: On-shell amplitude technique that reconstructs loop integrands by sewing tree amplitudes on multiple cuts; together with color-kinematics duality and the double copy it powers state-of-the-art supergravity UV computations.

nodes.name: Eleven-dimensional supergravity
nodes.definition: Unique maximal supergravity (Cremmer-Julia-Scherk 1978); the highest dimension supporting a consistent supergravity with spin <=2. Its dimensional reduction yields 4D N=8 supergravity, and it is the low-energy limit of M-theory.

nodes.name: Higher-derivative (quadratic) gravity
nodes.definition: Gravity action with curvature-squared terms (C^2, R^2); the propagator falls off as 1/p^4, making the theory power-counting renormalizable (Stelle 1977), but the Weyl-squared term introduces a massive spin-2 state with wrong-sign kinetic term (ghost).

nodes.name: Ostrogradsky ghost
nodes.definition: Generic instability of theories with more than two time derivatives: the Hamiltonian is unbounded below classically, and upon quantization the extra degree of freedom carries negative norm/energy, violating unitarity. The central obstruction for higher-derivative gravity.

nodes.name: Agravity (adimensional gravity)
nodes.definition: Salvio-Strumia scale-invariant gravity with only dimensionless couplings and a four-derivative graviton kinetic term; renormalizable and asymptotically free with no input mass scale, at the price of a physical ghost; predicts inflation from beta-functions.

nodes.name: Horava-Lifshitz gravity
nodes.definition: Gravity with anisotropic (Lifshitz) scaling t->b^z t, x->b x with z=3 in the UV, giving propagators ~1/(omega^2 - k^6) and power-counting renormalizability at the cost of breaking Lorentz invariance, which must re-emerge in the IR.

nodes.name: UV Lorentz-invariance violation
nodes.definition: Breaking of Lorentz symmetry at high momenta (as in Horava-Lifshitz), allowing improved UV behavior; radiative corrections threaten to leak the violation to low energies (tightly experimentally bounded), requiring fine-tuning, and a scalar mode suffers strong coupling.

nodes.name: Nonlocal / infinite-derivative ghost-free gravity
nodes.definition: Gravity whose kinetic operator contains an entire (root-free) function of the d'Alembertian, e.g. exp(-Box/M_s^2), exponentially suppressing the UV propagator and improving loop convergence without introducing new poles (no new ghosts); price is nonlocality.

nodes.name: Conformal (Weyl) gravity
nodes.definition: Gravity with action proportional to the Weyl tensor squared, invariant under local conformal rescalings; renormalizable but contains an Ostrogradsky ghost. In critical gravity / Maldacena's construction the massive ghost can be removed by boundary conditions.

nodes.name: Shape dynamics
nodes.definition: Reformulation of GR (Barbour, Gomes, Koslowski) that trades spacetime refoliation invariance for 3D local conformal (scale) invariance as a gauge symmetry, realizing a relational, Machian description of gravitational dynamics.

nodes.name: Fakeons / purely virtual particles
nodes.definition: Anselmi's prescription quantizing would-be ghosts as fake, strictly off-shell degrees of freedom that never appear as asymptotic in/out states; this preserves unitarity (in the limit of vanishing cosmological constant) while keeping renormalizability of quadratic gravity.

nodes.name: BPS-protected counterterm operators
nodes.definition: Supersymmetric invariants built from shortened (BPS) superconformal multiplets that could serve as candidate counterterms; in N=8 supergravity these BPS invariants fail to be E7(7)-invariant, removing protected counterterms below seven loops.

nodes.name: Swampland
nodes.definition: The (even larger) set of seemingly consistent semiclassical EFTs coupled to gravity that nonetheless admit NO ultraviolet completion into quantum gravity.
